anchors.title: Rougher interpretation can win
anchors.decision_rule: A rougher essay with a clearer interpretive claim, stronger explanation of theme or character, and more meaningful use of evidence should beat a polished plot summary.

anchors.title: Formula is not analysis
anchors.decision_rule: Do not reward five-paragraph control, repeated topic sentences, or tidy transitions over actual literary reasoning about how the text creates meaning.

anchors.title: Evidence must be explained
anchors.decision_rule: Specific events or quotations count most when the student explains how they support the interpretation; named events without explanation are weaker.

anchors.title: Alternate themes can win
anchors.decision_rule: Do not require both essays to use the same theme wording. A defensible theme about trauma, healing, identity, trust, accountability, consequences, or support can beat a repeated on-prompt keyword when it is better developed.

anchors.title: Sustained mechanism beats event listing
anchors.decision_rule: A sustained analysis of one important relationship, conflict, or mechanism of change can beat a broader but more summary-driven list of events.

anchors.title: Completion floor
anchors.decision_rule: An unfinished scaffold, outline, or fragmentary draft should not beat a complete essay unless the complete essay is off-task or meaning is not recoverable.

anchors.title: Mechanics are secondary until meaning breaks
anchors.decision_rule: Conventions should not bury a paper with substantially stronger interpretation unless the errors make the analysis hard to understand.

caution_checks: Which essay explains why the character, conflict, theme, or author choice matters?
Is one essay repeating an on-prompt keyword while the other develops a defensible alternate literary theme?
Is one essay sustaining analysis of an important relationship or mechanism rather than merely listing more events?
Is the cleaner essay mostly retelling events without a literary claim?
Is the rougher essay still intelligible enough that its stronger interpretation should count?
Is either response unfinished scaffold text that should trigger a completion floor?
